summary: ExpireMate MVP first-run testing. Backend: 34/34 pytest tests passed including auth (register/login/me/logout via both bearer & cookies), AI moderation (Oxycodone BLOCKED, Sealed Tylenol ALLOWED via Claude), full item lifecycle (verified gating → upload → list → claim with 4-digit code → confirm), Stripe verify/donate checkout creation (preset, custom range validation, payment_transactions persisted), donations stats/leaderboard shape, /api/files serving real uploaded image bytes from Emergent storage. Frontend: all 8 public routes (/, /browse, /how-it-works, /donate, /leaderboard, /safety, /login, /register) render. Donate page reaches /api/payments/donate-checkout and Stripe redirect occurs on $5 click. Register form renders all fields including 18+ terms; submission blocked in automation by a styled-card overlay intercepting the terms checkbox click — submission unverified.

ui_bugs.issue: Terms checkbox is wrapped by .em-card div which intercepts pointer events, making the checkbox un-clickable via normal click in automated tests (and possibly hard to tap on touch devices). Recommend pointer-events:none on the wrapper or moving the input to top of stacking context.

issues: Browser tab title is generic 'Emergent | Fullstack App' — should be 'ExpireMate' for branding.

action_items: Fix RegisterPage terms-checkbox click interception (overlay div blocking input).
Optional: Add data-testid attributes to interactive elements (donate amount buttons, Donate-via-Stripe, Verify, Create-account, login submit) — none were present, slows down testing.
Backend lower-cases email on save but returns lowercased — fine, but document for clients.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: stripe_webhook does not verify Stripe-Signature properly — relies on handle_webhook; signature should be required (currently swallows errors and returns ok:false silently).
server.py: get_item endpoint uses bare 'except Exception: pass' for auth — fine for optional auth but masks unexpected errors.

auth_utils.set_auth_cookies uses samesite='none', secure=True — correct for cross-site preview; CORS allow_origins falls back to ['*'] when FRONTEND_URL unset, which is incompatible with allow_credentials=True (browsers reject). Set FRONTEND_URL explicitly in prod.
server.py: random.randint for claim_code is not cryptographically random — use secrets.randbelow(10000) for better unpredictability.
Single-file server.py (~545 lines) approaching split threshold; consider routers (auth/items/payments/donations).

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (routes load, donate confirmed, register-submit blocked by overlay)

test_credentials: test_owner_<ts>@expiremate.com / Test1234 (created by tests; flipped verified=true directly via Mongo for post/claim flow). See /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Created ephemeral TEST_ prefixed users + 1 active→claimed→completed Sealed Tylenol item; uploaded sample JPEG bytes to Emergent storage. No cleanup script ran — minor residual TEST_ users in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is comprehensive — run it first. For frontend automation, the terms-checkbox on /register needs force=True click or a CSS fix. Donate flow can be confirmed via network response on /api/payments/donate-checkout. Don't try to complete Stripe payment — only verify URL is produced. Use Authorization: Bearer header (token in register/login response body) since cross-site cookies are unreliable in automation.
rca of the issue: RegisterPage submit blocked in tests: the .em-card wrapper div has pointer-events that intercept the checkbox; reproduce by going to /register and trying to .check() the terms input — Playwright reports 'div intercepts pointer events'. Workaround: page.locator('input[type=checkbox]').check(force=True) or page.evaluate to set checked. Real users likely unaffected on desktop but may be on touch.
